strengths: Completed all required phases including loop introduction, positioning, pusher advancement, and tail cut
Achieved excellent completion time of 42 seconds with no penalty deductions
Demonstrated smooth loop manipulation and controlled pusher advancement throughout the sequence
weaknesses: Loop positioning around the target mark could be more precise and deliberate
Pusher advancement technique shows some inconsistency in rhythm and control
Final tail cut execution could be more efficient with better instrument positioning

drills.name: Mark-centering drill
drills.description: Place the loop at the target mark, pause, and verify alignment before cinching. Practice 15 repetitions focusing on precise positioning.
drills.targets_weakness: Loop positioning around the target mark could be more precise and deliberate

drills.name: Loop opening and parking
drills.description: 10 repetitions of opening the loop and parking it around the target appendage before tightening, emphasizing controlled movements.
drills.targets_weakness: Pusher advancement technique shows some inconsistency in rhythm and control

drills.name: Tail-cut finish drill
drills.description: 10 secure placements followed by controlled tail cuts without disturbing the loop, focusing on optimal instrument angles.
drills.targets_weakness: Final tail cut execution could be more efficient with better instrument positioning

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid completion consistency into more precise positioning and smoother technique refinements. Your timing is excellent, now work on eliminating small inefficiencies in each phase.
